statement: The hamburger menu is always bad and should never be used.
why_it_spreads: Over-generalisation of valid findings that hidden navigation reduces discoverability, hardened into an absolute rule.
evidence_against: NN/g (src-nngroup, https://www.nngroup.com/articles/hamburger-menus/): hidden navigation lowers discoverability and increases task time, but on small screens space is constrained and the icon is now widely recognised.
The trade-off is contextual: visible navigation is better when space allows, but hidden navigation can be a reasonable compromise on mobile.
replacement_principle: Prefer visible or partially visible navigation when screen space permits; reserve hidden menus for secondary items or genuinely space-constrained mobile layouts, and keep the trigger conventional and accessible.
still_applies_when: On small viewports where there is no room for visible primary navigation
For secondary or overflow navigation that is not central to the main task
